skill.name: hyperframes-cli
skill.description: HyperFrames CLI tool — hyperframes init, lint, inspect, preview, render, transcribe, tts, doctor, browser, info, upgrade, compositions, docs, benchmark. Use when scaffolding a project, linting, validating, inspecting visual layout in compositions, previewing in the studio, rendering to video, transcribing audio, generating TTS, or troubleshooting the HyperFrames environment.
skill.summary: CLI tool for video rendering from HTML compositions using HyperFrames
skill.author: heygen-com

security_audit.summary: All 69 static findings are false positives. The external_command detections are markdown code examples showing CLI usage, not executable code. Network detections are localhost URLs for local development preview, which is expected. No cryptographic code or malicious patterns exist in this skill.

content.user_title: Render HTML compositions to video with HyperFrames CLI
content.value_statement: Transform HTML-based video compositions into MP4 or WebM output without manual rendering. Automate scaffolding, linting, visual inspection, and final delivery for content creators and development teams.
actual_capabilities: Scaffold new video projects with templates and example files
Lint HTML compositions for errors, warnings, and info messages
Inspect visual layout across timeline for text overflow and clipping issues
Preview compositions in browser with hot-reload and studio interface
Render compositions to MP4 or WebM with configurable quality and FPS
Transcribe audio files and import existing subtitle formats
limitations: Requires Node.js >= 22 and FFmpeg installed locally
Cannot render compositions without proper data-composition-id attributes
Visual inspection requires headless Chrome which may not work in all environments

use_cases.title: Automate video generation in CI/CD pipelines
use_cases.description: Use hyperframes render with --non-interactive flag to generate videos as part of automated workflows without manual intervention.

use_cases.title: Create marketing video content from templates
use_cases.description: Scaffold a project with a template like product-promo, customize the HTML composition, then render to final MP4 for social media or advertising.

use_cases.title: Debug and fix visual layout issues in video compositions
use_cases.description: Run hyperframes lint to catch structural errors, then hyperframes inspect to identify text overflow and clipping before final rendering.

prompt_templates.title: Scaffold a new video project
prompt_templates.prompt: Run `npx hyperframes init my-project --non-interactive` to create a new HyperFrames video project in the current directory.
prompt_templates.scenario: When user wants to start a new video project

prompt_templates.title: Validate and inspect a composition
prompt_templates.prompt: Run `npx hyperframes lint` followed by `npx hyperframes inspect --json` to check for errors and visual layout issues in the current composition.
prompt_templates.scenario: When preparing a composition for final rendering

prompt_templates.title: Render a composition to video
prompt_templates.prompt: Run `npx hyperframes render --output final.mp4 --quality high --fps 30` to render the current composition to a high-quality MP4 file.
prompt_templates.scenario: When ready to export the final video

prompt_templates.title: Preview and test changes live
prompt_templates.prompt: Run `npx hyperframes preview --port 3002` to start the preview server and open the HyperFrames studio for interactive testing.

output_examples.output:
Checking environment...
✓ Chrome: installed
✓ FFmpeg: installed
✓ Node: v22.10.0
✓ Memory: 8GB available

Environment is ready for rendering.

Linting composition...
✓ index.html: valid
✗ compositions/intro.html: missing data-composition-id
⚠ compositions/outro.html: overlapping tracks at 2.5s

1 error, 1 warning found.

best_practices: Run lint and inspect commands before previewing to catch structural and visual issues early
Use --docker flag for reproducible, byte-identical renders across different environments
Use draft quality during iteration and switch to high quality only for final delivery
anti_patterns: Do not skip lint and inspect steps before rendering - structural errors may cause silent failures
Do not use 60fps unless specifically needed - it doubles render time with minimal visual benefit
Do not render directly to final output during development - use draft quality to save time

faq.question: What are the system requirements for HyperFrames CLI?
faq.answer: Node.js version 22 or higher and FFmpeg must be installed. Run npx hyperframes doctor to verify your environment.

faq.question: How do I render a video without the preview window?
faq.answer: Use npx hyperframes render with --non-interactive flag in CI/CD environments or automated scripts.

faq.question: What is the difference between lint and inspect commands?
faq.answer: Lint checks structural errors like missing attributes and overlapping tracks. Inspect runs the composition in headless Chrome to detect visual issues like text overflow.

faq.question: How do I ensure consistent renders across different machines?
faq.answer: Use the --docker flag which runs rendering inside a Docker container with fixed Chrome and FFmpeg versions.

faq.question: Can I add audio narration to my video composition?
faq.answer: Yes, use npx hyperframes tts to generate text-to-speech, and npx hyperframes transcribe to import existing audio or subtitle files.

faq.question: What quality settings should I use for final delivery?
faq.answer: Use --quality high --fps 30 for final delivery. Use --quality draft during iteration to save time.
